AddApplication.document: This API is used to create an application
AddApplication.name: Create an application

AddHardware.document: Add hardware devices, generate inactive hardware devices, and support batch addition
AddHardware.name: Add hardware device

CreateEncryptedKey.document: This API is used to configure and refresh preset keys.
CreateEncryptedKey.name: Set or update a key

DeleteApplication.document: This API is used to delete applications
DeleteApplication.name: Deletes applications

DownloadActiveDeviceCount.document: Download the number of active devices statistics
DownloadActiveDeviceCount.name: Download the number of active devices statistics

GetApplication.document: This API is used to query applications.
GetApplication.name: This API is used to obtain application information.

GetDestIPByName.document: Statistics of a single device accessing the target IP address
GetDestIPByName.name: Query target IP addresses based on device name

GetDevice.document: This API is used to search device details by specified device ID.
GetDevice.name: Retrieve device details

GetDevicePayMode.document: This API is used to obtain the payment mode of a device.
GetDevicePayMode.name: Obtain the payment mode of a device

GetDevices.document: This API is used to get device information list.
GetDevices.name: List of Device Information

GetFlowAlarmInfo.document: This API is used to query user traffic alarm settings based on AppId, including threshold, callback url and key.
GetFlowAlarmInfo.name: Query traffic alarm info

GetFlowPackages.document: Retrieve the data transfer plan list
GetFlowPackages.name: Retrieve the Data Transfer Plan List

GetFlowStatistic.document: Retrieve traffic usage data for a specified device Id at a specified time point.
GetFlowStatistic.name: Retrieve data traffic stats

GetFlowStatisticByGroup.document: Retrieve traffic usage data for the specified group and time period
GetFlowStatisticByGroup.name: Retrieve data traffic stats based on device group

GetFlowStatisticByName.document: Retrieve traffic usage data for a specified device Id at a specified time point.
GetFlowStatisticByName.name: Retrieve data traffic stats based on device name

GetFlowStatisticByRegion.document: Retrieve traffic usage data for the specified region and time point
GetFlowStatisticByRegion.name: Retrieve data traffic stats by region

GetGroupList.document: This API is used to obtain a group list.
GetGroupList.name: This API is used to obtain a group list.

GetHardwareInfo.document: This API is used to get hardware device information.
GetHardwareInfo.name: Retrieve hardware device information

GetHardwareList.document: This API is used to get the hardware list of the manufacturer.
GetHardwareList.name: Get the hardware list of the manufacturer

GetL3ConnList.document: Retrieve the list of interconnection rules
GetL3ConnList.name: Retrieve the interconnection rule list

GetMonitorDataByName.document: This API is used to obtain the download file URL for all monitoring metrics of a single device.
GetMonitorDataByName.name: Download the monitoring data file based on device name

GetMultiFlowStatistic.document: Obtain batch device traffic statistics curves
GetMultiFlowStatistic.name: Obtain batch device traffic statistics

GetNetMonitor.document: This API is used to obtain real-time traffic statistics per device.
GetNetMonitor.name: Retrieve traffic monitoring information

GetNetMonitorByName.document: This API is used to obtain real-time traffic statistics per device.
GetNetMonitorByName.name: Retrieve traffic monitoring information based on device name

GetPublicKey.document: This API is used to access the public key for signature verification.
GetPublicKey.name: Access the public key.

GetStatisticData.document: Download traffic data on the usage statistics page
GetStatisticData.name: Download usage statistics

GetStatisticDataByName.document: Download traffic data on the usage statistics page
GetStatisticDataByName.name: Download usage statistics based on device name

GetVendorHardware.document: This API is used to get the hardware device list of the manufacturer.
GetVendorHardware.name: Get the hardware device list of the manufacturer

GroupAddDevice.document: Add device to already exist group
GroupAddDevice.name: Adds a device to a group

GroupDeleteDevice.name: Deletes a device in the group

ModifyPackageRenewFlag.document: Auto renewal of data transfer plans can be enabled or disabled, unaffected by ongoing effective plans in the current cycle.
ModifyPackageRenewFlag.name: Modify the data transfer plan auto-renewal flag

OrderFlowPackage.name: Subscribe to a data transfer plan

ReportOrder.document: Users report custom order information, and the Multiple Network Acceleration service saves the information related to.
ReportOrder.name: Report order information

SetNotifyUrl.document: This API is used to set user traffic alarm information. Use this API setting to configure the data transfer plan alarm threshold as well as the callback url and key when an alarm is generated.
SetNotifyUrl.name: Set user traffic alarm info

UpdateL3Conn.name: Update the remark of an interconnection rule

members.document: Access environment. 0: public cloud gateway; 1: private gateway; 2: public cloud gateway and private gateway. By default if left blank, public cloud gateway is selected. Specific meaning: Public cloud gateway: device can only integrate with public cloud gateway (nearby access). Private gateway: device can only integrate with private gateway that has been launched (nearby access or fixed ip). Public cloud gateway and private gateway: device can also integrate with public cloud gateway and private gateway that has been launched (nearby access or fixed ip).
members.name: AccessScope

members.document:
When the payer is the tenant, the tenant license payment method is available.
Monthly authorization
1: Permanent license
If not passed, it defaults to monthly authorization.
This parameter is invalid when the manufacturer is the payer.
members.name: LicensePayMode

members.document: Device group name. Reserved parameter. GroupId must be imported when grouping is required.
members.name: GroupName

members.document: No traffic processing method for the device. 0: pay-as-you-go, 1: truncate and accelerate
members.name: FlowTrunc

members.document: The unique request ID, generated by the server, will be returned for every request (if the request fails to reach the server for other reasons, the request will not obtain a RequestId). RequestId is required for locating a problem.
members.name: RequestId

members.document: Application name of the created app
members.name: MpApplicationName

members.document: base64 key string of the new application, optional, automatically generated by the system if not filled
members.name: MpApplicationKey

members.document: Access environment. 0: public cloud gateway; 1: private gateway; 2: public cloud gateway and private gateway. By default if left blank, public cloud gateway is selected. Specific meaning: Public cloud gateway: The device can only connect to public cloud gateway (Proximity access). Private gateway: The device can only connect to already launched private gateway (Proximity access or fixed ip access). Public cloud gateway and private gateway: The device can also connect to both public cloud gateway and already launched private gateway (Proximity access or fixed ip access).
members.name: AccessScope

members.document: base64-format key encrypted with the encryption algorithm
members.name: MpApplicationKey

members.document: The unique request ID, generated by the server, will be returned for every request (if the request fails to reach the server for other reasons, the request will not obtain a RequestId). RequestId is required for locating a problem.
members.name: RequestId

members.document: base64-encoded key string of the new device, optional. If not filled, automatically generated by the system.
members.name: DataKey

members.document:
Whether to set the preset key.
true: set preset key.
false: Leave the preset key unset.
members.name: Encrypted

members.document:
Access environment. 0: public cloud gateway; 1: private gateway; 2: public cloud gateway and private gateway. By default if left blank, public cloud gateway.
specific meaning
Public cloud gateway: The device can only integrate with the public cloud gateway (nearby access).
Private gateway: The device can only integrate with the already launched private gateway (nearby access or fixed ip integration).
Public cloud gateway and private gateway: The device can also integrate with the public cloud gateway and the already launched private gateway (connected to nearby or fixed ip access).
members.name: AccessScope

members.document:
license payment method 
Monthly authorization 
Permanent license 
If not specified, it defaults to monthly authorization. To permanently license a device, you need to call the OrderPerLicense API to pay the authorization fee. Otherwise, the device cannot be used.
members.name: LicensePayMode

members.document: Access environment. 0: Public cloud gateway; 1: Private gateway; 2: Public cloud gateway and private gateway. Default is public cloud gateway. Specific meaning: Public cloud gateway: The device can only connect to the public cloud gateway (nearby access). Private gateway: The device can only connect to the already launched private gateway (nearby access or fixed ip access). Public cloud gateway and private gateway: The device can also connect to both the public cloud gateway and the already launched private gateway (nearby access or fixed ip access).
members.name: AccessScope

members.document: Authorization validity period of license 0: Monthly authorization 1: Permanent license
members.name: LicensePayMode

members.document: No traffic processing method for the device. 0: pay-as-you-go, 1: truncate and accelerate
members.name: FlowTrunc

members.document: Downstream real-time rate (floating-point type replaces the integer type of the previous version DataRx).
members.name: DownRate

members.document: Uplink real-time rate (floating-point type replaces the integer of the previous version TxRate)
members.name: UpRate

members.document:
Payment mode.
Prepaid Traffic Package
0: Pay-as-you-go by traffic
members.name: PayMode

members.document: Traffic package ID. Only available when the payment mode is package type.
members.name: ResourceId

members.document: Query granularity. Valid values: 0: day, 1: week, 2: month. Default is day.
members.name: Period

members.document: device group, if not passed, query all
members.name: DevGroup

members.document: License type. If not passed, query all. 1: Tenant monthly payment, 2: Manufacturer monthly payment, 3: Permanent license.

members.document: The unique request ID, generated by the server, will be returned for every request (if the request fails to reach the server for other reasons, the request will not obtain a RequestId). RequestId is required for locating a problem.
members.name: RequestId

members.document: Maximum value of traffic (measurement unit: bytes)
members.name: MaxValue

members.document: Unique resource ID of the data transfer plan
members.name: ResourceId

members.document: User AppId belonging to the data transfer plan
members.name: AppId

members.document:
Traffic package specification type. Values are as follows:
DEVICE_1_FLOW_20G,DEVICE_2_FLOW_50G,
DEVICE_3_FLOW_100G,
DEVICE_5_FLOW_500G represents traffic packages of 20G, 50G, 100G, and 500G gear selection.
Gear selection also impacts the bandwidth cap of the data transfer plan.
20G: Bind up to 1 device.
50G: Bind up to 2 devices.
100G: Bind up to 3 devices.
500G: Bind up to 5 devices.
members.name: PackageType

members.document: Traffic packet status. 0: Inactive, 1: Within validity period, 2: Expired.
members.name: Status

members.document: Purchase time, Unix timestamp format, unit: second
members.name: CreateTime

members.name: ActiveTime

members.document: Expiration time, Unix Timestamp Format, unit: second
members.name: ExpireTime

members.name: DeviceList

members.document: Remaining data transfer plan, unit: MB
members.name: CapacityRemain

members.document: Auto-renewal flag. true represents auto-renewal, false represents no auto-renewal.
members.name: RenewFlag

members.document: Resource package change status: 0: No change; 1: Changing; 2: Changed or renewed
members.name: ModifyStatus

members.document: Traffic truncation flag. true to enable traffic truncation, false to disable traffic truncation.
members.name: TruncFlag

members.document: Precise remaining data transfer plan, unit: MB
members.name: CapacityRemainPrecise

members.document: Query granularity. Valid values: 0: day, 1: week, 2: month. Default is day.

members.document: Access region. Value ranges from 'MC' to 'AM'. MC=Chinese mainland AP=Asia Pacific EU=Europe AM=Americas. Leave it blank to represent all regions.
members.name: AccessRegion

members.document: Gateway type. 0: public cloud gateway; 1: private gateway. Default is 0 if not specified.
members.name: GatewayType

members.document: Device name list. Up to 10 devices. Used when downloading traffic volume of multiple devices. At this point, DeviceName can be "-1".
members.name: DeviceList

members.document: Device group ID. If not specified, do not pass it. Used when downloading data by grouping.
members.name: GroupId

members.document: Application ID. Leave blank if no designated application is specified. Used when downloading data by app.
members.name: MpApplicationId

members.document:
License billing mode: 1. Tenant monthly payment 2. Manufacturer monthly payment 3. Permanent license.
Note: This parameter will be subsequently deprecated. Please use LicensePayMode and Payer for new access.

Authorization validity period of license 
0: Monthly authorization 
1: Permanent license
members.name: LicensePayMode

members.name: FlowTrunc

members.document: Unique resource ID of the data transfer plan
members.name: ResourceId

members.document: Auto-renewal flag. true represents auto-renewal, false represents no auto-renewal.
members.name: RenewFlag

members.document: The unique request ID, generated by the server, will be returned for every request (if the request fails to reach the server for other reasons, the request will not obtain a RequestId). RequestId is required for locating a problem.
members.name: RequestId

members.document:
Traffic package specification type. Values are as follows:
DEVICE_1_FLOW_20G,DEVICE_2_FLOW_50G,
DEVICE_3_FLOW_100G,
DEVICE_5_FLOW_500G represents traffic packages of 20G, 50G, 100G, and 500G gear selection.
Gear selection also impacts the bandwidth cap of the data transfer plan.
20G: Bind up to 1 device.
50G: Bind up to 2 devices.
100G: Bind up to 3 devices.
500G: Bind up to 5 devices.
members.name: PackageType

members.document:
Device ID list bound to the data transfer plan. Number of bundled instances depends on the specification tiers of the plan.
20G: Bind up to 1 device.
50G: Bind up to 2 devices.
100G: Bind up to 3 devices.
500G: Bind up to 5 devices.
members.name: DeviceList

members.document: Whether the service is automatically renewed conflicts with traffic truncation. You can only enable one option.
members.name: AutoRenewFlag

members.document: Region flag. 0: Chinese mainland, 1: outside the Chinese mainland
members.name: PackageRegion

members.document: Whether traffic truncation is enabled. This option conflicts with auto renewal.
members.name: FlowTruncFlag

members.document:
Whether to automatically select voucher. Default false.
Selection strategy for multiple vouchers: Deduct vouchers by this priority - voucher that can offset the total amount of the Payment Order, voucher with the earliest Expiration, voucher with the maximum deductible amount, voucher with the minimum balance, cash voucher. Only one voucher can be deducted at most.
members.name: AutoVoucher

members.document:
Designated voucher ID. This parameter is invalid when selecting voucher automatically. Only one voucher can be input.
Note: If the designated voucher does not meet the order deduction conditions, proceed with normal payment without deducting the voucher.

members.document: Access environment. 0: public cloud gateway; 1: private gateway; 2: public cloud gateway and private gateway. By default if left blank, public cloud gateway is selected. Specific meaning: Public cloud gateway: The device can only connect to public cloud gateway (Proximity access). Private gateway: The device can only connect to already launched private gateway (Proximity access or fixed ip access). Public cloud gateway and private gateway: The device can also connect to both public cloud gateway and already launched private gateway (Proximity access or fixed ip access).
members.name: AccessScope
